sample.title: Can Cuba’s Economy Survive Trump?
sample.description:
Donald Trump’s blockade of Cuba, condemned by international groups, has caused shortages of fuel, food and medicine. Under threat from the US, will Cuba’s authoritarian government bend to his will? And as Havana seeks to rebuild a shattered economy, will it turn away from socialism and toward capitalism?

Read more on Bloomberg.com:

Cuba’s Bold Free-Market Turn Fails to Convince US It’s Ready to Change
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/cuba-economic-crisis-trump-unconvinced-by-free-market-reforms?srnd=undefined

Watch more Weekly Docs: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLqq4LnWs3olXOhUOv4O6r-9OdWTQN83se

Everything you need to know about the big topics shaping our world, direct from Bloomberg's global newsroom.

sample.title: Why Off-Season Travel Is Disappearing
sample.description:
As summers are growing hotter and crowds are getting bigger, more Americans are choosing to visit international destinations in the early spring and fall. Airlines are betting on more people taking long-haul flights during shoulder seasons and are offering more international flights year-round. CNBC's Leslie Josephs has more.

Chapters:
0:00 Crowds and heat are driving travelers away
1:05 Airlines are offering more international flights
1:44 International flights are more lucrative

Produced, Shot and Edited by: Zach Green
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Reporter: Leslie Josephs
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.description:

sample.title: Who Is Funding Pres. Trump’s Freedom 250 Celebrations?
sample.description:
CNBC's Luke Fountain is at the Great American State Fair on the Washington Mall for celebrations of the nation's 250th Independence Day. Freedom 250, the sponsor of the state fair, has become politicized because the Trump administration has pushed many events through that, rather than America 250, a bipartisan group created by Congress. It’s raised concerns from ethics watchdogs and Democrats.

Reporter Luke Fountain
Produced, Shot and Edited by Carlos Waters
Production Assistant Irit Skulnik
Senior Director of Video Lindsey Jacobson
Additional Footage: Getty Images
Additional Sources: Natural Resources Committee Democrats, America250.org, Public Employees for Environmental Responsibility, National Park Foundation

sample.title: Apple closing in on Nvidia to become world's most-valuable company
sample.description: Apple nears an all-time high, closing in on Nvidia to become the world's most-valuable company. CNBC's MacKenzie Sigalos has the details.

sample.title: U.S. revokes Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks
sample.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department will revoke its authorization of Iranian oil sales after a series of attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

The Office of Foreign Assets Control is revoking the license that allowed the production, delivery, and sale of Iranian oil through August 21, a U.S. official told CNBC Tuesday.

The Trump administration waived U.S. sanctions on Iranian oil under the interim deal to reopen Hormuz. But Iran has launched a series of attacks on tankers transiting the strait this week, despite promising safe passages to commercial ships under the deal.

sample.title: Small caps' pace of outperformance will moderate through year end, says Goldman's Tim Urbanowicz

sample.title: United Airlines CEO Scot Kirby on why freedom is America's superpower
sample.description: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby reflects on the entrepreneurial spirit that shaped the nation’s founding and explains why freedom continues to be one of America’s greatest competitive advantages.

sample.title: Prosecutors say video shows suspect in sniper position as Charlie Kirk was shot
sample.description:
Utah prosecutors showed video that an investigator said captured the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk last year in a sniper's position on the roof of a university building from which the prominent conservative activist was shot.

The evidence was presented during the second day of a preliminary hearing in Provo, Utah, in which prosecutors are trying to convince District Court Judge Tony Graf to bring Tyler Robinson to trial.

sample.title: Eighteen injured in Damascus explosions near hotel where Macron was staying. #Syria #BBCNews
Nigel Farage resigns as MP | BBC Newscast
sample.description:
Today Nigel Farage resigns as an MP, triggering a by-election in Clacton which he says he plans to stand in. He is currently facing an ongoing parliamentary standards investigation into whether he should have declared a £5 million gift he received from the cryptocurrency billionaire Christopher Harborne.

Making the announcement in a statement streamed online, Farage said that he had done “nothing wrong” and that he needed the money to pay for security. He accused Sky News of harassing his family and said that standards regulations had been weaponised by the “establishment.” Calling the by-election “people versus the establishment”, Mr Farage said “the people of Clacton should be the judges of my actions.”

Adam, Alex and Chris unpack what the move might mean.

00:00 Intro
00:34 Why is Nigel Farage resigning as MP?
01:56 Farage's announcement
07:49 Who else is standing in Clacton?
14:20 Will Burnham and Farage compete for our attention?
20:14 A new side of Nigel Farage?

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news
